title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Client Identity, and Proxy Trust · Go Semantics Case: Host Allowlist Rebinding
summary: An origin accepts arbitrary Host values, constructs password-reset links from untrusted forwarded fields, rate-limits a proxy address, and logs raw targets containing secrets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo host allowlist rebinding increment in request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate authoritative host and port against a configured allowlist before routing, link construction, cache keys, or tenant selection.
Interpret Method, URL, Host, RequestURI, Header, Body, Trailer, RemoteAddr, TLS, Pattern, and Context using server-side semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
